source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Material Handling with Tugger

actions_taken: Task Based Risk Assessment (TBRA) reviewed and signed
Step by Step Procedure (SSP) form reviewed and signed
Incident documentation, photos and witness statements collected
event.summary: On February 21, 2022, during wire rope replacement operations on the Seadrill West Vela drillship, a 114-foot wire rope fell to the drill floor when rigging came free while crew was attempting to secure it to drum clamps. The incident occurred because the drill crew failed to follow the Task Based Risk Assessment procedure, which specified using a 1-3/8" clamp for rigging. Instead, they used an uncertified nylon strap with multiple half hitches. The required clamp was unavailable. No injuries or equipment damage resulted.
recommendations: Update appropriate maintenance procedure with proper manufacturer procedure
Ensure proper wire snakes are available onboard for wire rope replacement
Update TBRA and SSP forms to reflect changes in manufacturer's procedure

hazards.name: Suspended load during wire rope handling

threats.name: Rigging failure due to inadequate securing method

consequences.name: Dropped object striking personnel or equipment
Injury or fatality from falling wire rope

controls.name: Task Based Risk Assessment (TBRA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and specify safe procedures

supporting_text: A Pre-job meeting was held and a 'Task Based Risk Assessment' (TBRA) and a 'Step by Step Procedure' (SSP) form was reviewed and signed by all personnel involved with this process.
The rigging procedure utilizing the nylon strap was not carried out as per the TBRA. The procedure calls for the use of a 1-3/8" clamp connected to the dead-end side of the wire rope.

controls.name: Specified rigging clamp (1-3/8" clamp)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to secure wire rope during handling
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturer specification for safe wire rope handling

supporting_text: The procedure calls for the use of a 1-3/8" clamp connected to the dead-end side of the wire rope.
The drill crew was unable to locate that clamp but proceeded anyway, using several wraps and multiple half hitches of the nylon strap to the new wire rope to hoist into place.
1-3/8" clamp was unavailable as the procedure specified.

controls.name: Certified rigging slings and equipment
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure rigging equipment integrity

supporting_text: The nylon strap used in this process was not located in the Seadrill onboard sling registry and had no inspection certifications.
It was not a Seadrill sling issued by any of their vendors and it is unclear where the sling originated.

controls.name: Supervision and work observation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to monitor compliance with procedures

supporting_text: The supervisor made the decision to replace the damaged wire rope with a new one.
The drill crew proceeded to disconnect the lower thimble on the damaged cable and connect it to the new cable... the drill crew was unable to locate that clamp but proceeded anyway

controls.name: Manufacturer's wire rope replacement procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control specifying safe work methods

supporting_text: Failure to follow manufacturer's wire change procedure.
Seadrill action plans are to update the appropriate maintenance procedure with the proper manufacturer procedure

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
